content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Jack Feller 
Oceanside City Councilman 
2806 Brandeis Drive 
Oceanside, California 92056 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-11-144 
Dear Councilman Feller: 
August 22, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1 dated August 5, 2011. While the Act requires 
the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in 
question (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a)), the Commission may decline to provide 
advice if a response relates to past conduct, or would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the 
public interest. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A),(F) and (c)(4)(A),(G).) 
After a careful review of the facts you have provided, we decline to provide written 
advice to you at this time because the advice you have sought involves past conduct and there is 
a pending Enforcement action regarding the situation described in your request. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K Brar 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8l000 through 9l0 14. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 181 IO through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
